sections.summary: Applies the Standard to commercial varieties of mangosteens (Garcinia mangostana L.) supplied fresh to consumers after preparation and packaging, excluding mangosteens intended for industrial processing.

Specifies overall quality provisions for mangosteens, including minimum requirements (whole, calyx/pedicel intact, sound, clean, pest-free, no abnormal moisture/smell/taste, fresh appearance, free of latex/pronounced blemishes, and cut-openable) and classification into quality classes with associated allowances and tolerances.

Lists the minimum quality requirements applicable to all classes of mangosteens: whole with calyx and pedicel intact, sound, clean, practically free of pests and pest damage, free of abnormal external moisture, foreign smell/taste, latex and pronounced blemishes, fresh in appearance with characteristic shape/colour/taste, and able to be cut open normally.

sections.section: 2.1.1  The development and condition of the mangosteens must be such as to enable them:
sections.summary: Requires mangosteens to be developed and conditioned so they can continue ripening to appropriate ripeness (skin at least pink), withstand transport and handling, and arrive in satisfactory condition at destination.

Classifies mangosteens into three classes and defines 'Extra' (superior quality, essentially defect-free) and 'Class I' (good quality allowing slight shape or surface defects) with specified limits so defects do not affect appearance, quality, keeping quality or presentation.

'Extra' Class: mangosteens of superior quality, characteristic of variety/commercial type, free of defects except very slight superficial ones that do not affect general appearance, quality, keeping quality or presentation in the package.

Class I: mangosteens of good quality, characteristic of variety/commercial type, permitting slight defects in shape and slight peel or calyx damage (bruising, scratches or other mechanical damage) provided the total affected area does not exceed 10% and defects do not affect the pulp, appearance, keeping quality or presentation.
Defines sizing by weight or equatorial diameter with size codes A–E and corresponding ranges: A 30–50 g/38–45 mm; B 51–75 g/46–52 mm; C 76–100 g/53–58 mm; D 101–125 g/59–62 mm; E >125 g/>62 mm.

Allows tolerances per package or lot for produce not meeting class or size requirements, specifying quality tolerances for classes and a 10% size tolerance by number or weight for produce falling within the immediately adjacent size.

Quality tolerances: for 'Extra' class up to 5% by number/weight may not meet Extra but must meet Class I or its tolerances, and for Class I up to 10% by number/weight may fail to meet the class or minimum requirements, excluding produce affected by rotting or otherwise unfit for consumption.
'Extra' Class tolerance: up to 5% by number or weight of mangosteens in a package may not satisfy Extra class requirements but must meet Class I or, exceptionally, fall within Class I tolerances.

Class I tolerance: up to 10% by number or weight of mangosteens may fail to satisfy Class I or the minimum requirements, except for produce affected by rotting or other deterioration making it unfit for consumption.

Size tolerances: for all classes, up to 10% by number or weight of mangosteens in a package or lot may not meet the declared size but must fall within the size immediately above or below the specified range.

Sets presentation requirements: packages/lots must be uniform and representative, and mangosteens must be packed to protect them using new, clean materials (non-toxic inks/glues allowed) and containers meeting the Recommended International Code of Practice (CAC/RCP 44-1995) with suitable quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance and free of foreign matter and smell.
Uniformity: each package or lot must contain only mangosteens of the same origin, quality and size, and the visible portion must represent the entire contents.

Packaging: mangosteens must be packed to protect the produce using new, clean materials that do not damage the fruit and printed with non-toxic ink or glue if applicable, in containers complying with CAC/RCP 44-1995 and meeting required quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance, with packages free of foreign matter and smell.

Requires containers for mangosteens to meet quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance criteria to ensure proper handling, shipping and preservation, and specifies that packages or bulk lots must be free of all foreign matter and odours.

Sets out labelling/marking requirements for consumer and non-retail packages supplementing the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1‑1985), and outlines particulars required on non‑retail containers (identification, nature, origin, commercial identification and optional official inspection mark).
Heading only: indicates specific additional provisions apply to consumer packages in addition to CODEX STAN 1‑1985 but contains no substantive details here.
Heading only: 'Nature of Produce' subheading with no substantive provisions in this section.

Requires non‑retail packages to bear specified particulars legibly and indelibly grouped on the same side and visible from outside (or on accompanying documents for bulk consignments), and introduces the required particulars covered in subsequent subclauses (identification, nature, origin, commercial identification, inspection mark).
Requires non‑retail packages to show the name and address of the exporter, packer and/or dispatcher, with an optional identification code.
Requires the name of the produce on packages when contents are not visible and allows optional declaration of variety or commercial type.
Requires declaration of the country of origin and optionally the district or national/regional/local place name.
Specifies commercial identification must include class, size (size code or minimum and maximum weight or length in grams or millimetres) and optionally net weight.

Addresses the optional official inspection mark, notes that packaging may include recycled food‑grade material, and states that where national law requires explicit name/address a code mark is acceptable only if accompanied by a clear reference to 'packer and/or dispatcher' (or equivalent) adjacent to the code.

Mandates that the produce comply with Codex maximum levels for contaminants and toxins (CODEX STAN 193‑1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

Recommends preparing and handling the produce in accordance with Codex Codes of Hygienic Practice (including CAC/RCP 1‑1969 and CAC/RCP 53‑2003) and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established under CAC/GL 21‑1997.
